description_long: This object is a large, hollowed stone with an irregular, organic shape and a rough, matte surface. Its exterior is predominantly a muted greenish-gray, with subtle, natural-looking mottling and faint polygonal patterns reminiscent of weathered rock or turtle shell. The opening is wide and uneven, with thick, rounded edges that suggest natural erosion or intentional carving. The interior is dark and cavernous, contrasting with the lighter, textured exterior, and the stone appears to be quite thick-walled. The surface shows signs of age and exposure, with minor undulations and a generally rugged appearance. This stone could serve as a natural receptacle or shelter in an urban park or landscaped area.
description: A large, hollow, greenish-gray stone with a rough, weathered surface and a wide, uneven opening. Its thick walls and natural mottling give it an organic, aged appearance.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the wide, irregular opening and the dark, cavernous interior.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thick, curved wall and the polygonal surface pattern.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the closed, rounded back and the mottled, textured exterior.
description_view_3: Angled view emphasizing the depth of the opening and the thickness of the stone's edge.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on the ground
attribute_pattern: subtle polygonal and mottled patterns, irregular and natural in appearance
attribute_weathering: rough and weathered, with minor undulations and signs of natural erosion
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: blends naturally with landscaped terrain, potentially partially surrounded by grass or low vegetation
